nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: SQL now extracts child task session metadata and joins child session activity for deterministic classification.
nonblocking_findings.why: Provides structural evidence needed to avoid repairing task tool parts that are still waiting on active child sessions.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: Regression covers active child task wait as live and stale child task as repairable.
nonblocking_findings.why: Directly locks prior blocker behavior with deterministic timestamps and parent-orphan liveness.

Verification passed: typecheck, lint, format:check, focused OpenCode session-debt vitest (16 tests), opencode-session-debt test file, status/formatter focused tests, spec JSON parse. adv_change_validate strict passed with no errors and one NO_DELTAS warning because specs were edited directly as in-repo law.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User accepted delivered work via chat: accept. Review verdict APPROVED after remediation. Contract matrix has 18/18 passed/respected; executive summary persisted; preview URL not_applicable.

items.text: Dry-run reports stale blank assistant ghosts and stale `running`/`pending` tool parts with bounded session/message/part/tool/status/age/context samples.
Apply mode refuses without explicit apply and backup directory, and writes DB/WAL/SHM backups before mutation.
Apply repairs only classified stale orphan tool parts in place: `state.status:'error'`, schema-valid error payload, `metadata.interrupted:true`, terminal timestamp.
Live/in-flight, idle-active, and plausibly task-waiting tool parts remain unchanged unless orphan evidence is strong.
Blank assistant ghost repair remains limited to classified orphan ghosts.
Parent assistant is completed only when all child parts are terminal; partial parents remain open.
Status output, docs/specs, and tests cover detection, exclusions, backup requirement, mutation shape, partial parent behavior, task-tool safety, and status output.
Never mutate a real OpenCode DB without explicit apply mode and populated backup.
Use deterministic classification over heuristic cleanup.
Preserve current blank assistant ghost repair safety boundaries.
Keep repaired tool-part payloads compatible with OpenCode tool-state schema.
Runtime watchdog implementation inside OpenCode is out of scope.
No cascade-delete repair for tool parts in this change.
No arbitrary non-schema fields inside OpenCode `state` payloads.

rows.evidence: Focused vitest: src/utils/opencode-session-debt.test.ts + status/formatter tests passed; scanner/status expose blank ghosts and stale tool-part counts/samples.
rows.notes: Dry-run/status reporting implemented with bounded samples and counts.

rows.evidence: opencode-session-debt.test.ts doctor apply fixture passed; apply without --backup-dir throws; backup_files asserted populated.
rows.notes: Doctor apply is explicit and backup-gated.

rows.evidence: opencode-session-debt.test.ts doctor apply fixture passed; repaired part has state.status error, metadata.interrupted true, state.time.end number.
rows.notes: In-place repair only for classified repairable tool parts.

rows.evidence: Post-review regression passed: task tools with active child sessions stay live/not repairable; live row remains running in doctor fixture.
rows.notes: Reviewer re-review APPROVED after task-tool child liveness fix.

rows.evidence: Blank assistant classifier tests passed; getDeletableBlankAssistantIds returns only orphan_ghost rows.
Doctor apply fixture passed; complete parent finish set to error, partial parent finish remains null when live child remains running.
rows.notes: Parent completion conditional on all child parts terminal.

rows.evidence: Status/formatter focused tests passed; spec JSON parse passed; docs/spec updated for rq-opencodeDebt01; lint/typecheck/format passed.
rows.notes: Coverage includes tests, docs/spec, and status output.

rows.evidence: Git diff touches repo files only; no ~/.local/bin path edits.
Doctor mutation path requires --apply --backup-dir and backupDatabaseFiles before applyRepairs; tests use temp DB only.
Classification uses SQL fields, timestamps, session/child-session liveness, deterministic thresholds; no heuristic-only repair authority.
Blank assistant APIs retained; tests for existing blank assistant behavior passed.
Repair writes runtime MessageV2 ToolPart-compatible state: status error, error string, metadata, time start/end; no arbitrary state.interrupted.
No OpenCode runtime watchdog implementation added; changes are ADV doctor/status/spec only.
Reviewer APPROVED after remediation; SQL deletes remain limited to validated blank assistant ids and tool parts use UPDATE.
No vacuum/compaction behavior added; WAL checkpoint + DB/WAL/SHM copy only.
Tool-part repair uses UPDATE part; no parent cascade-delete for tool repair.
Repair marker is state.metadata.interrupted, not arbitrary top-level state.interrupted.

acceptanceCriteria: Dry-run reports stale blank assistant ghosts and stale `running`/`pending` tool parts with bounded session/message/part/tool/status/age/context samples.
Apply mode refuses without explicit apply and backup directory, and writes DB/WAL/SHM backups before mutation.
Apply repairs only classified stale orphan tool parts in place: `state.status:'error'`, schema-valid error payload, `metadata.interrupted:true`, terminal timestamp.
Live/in-flight, idle-active, and plausibly task-waiting tool parts remain unchanged unless orphan evidence is strong.
Blank assistant ghost repair remains limited to classified orphan ghosts.
Parent assistant is completed only when all child parts are terminal; partial parents remain open.
Status output, docs/specs, and tests cover detection, exclusions, backup requirement, mutation shape, partial parent behavior, task-tool safety, and status output.

clarify_findings.message: Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
Success criteria are placeholder or empty. What measurable outcomes define done?
